texto: No one, from pontiffs to professors, has a monopoly on the truth. In the end, we are all just travelers--not scientists or mystics or any one brand of thinker. By nature, we are scientists and mystics, reductionists and holists, left-brained and right-brained, mixed up creatures trying to catch an occasional glimpse of the truth. The best we can do is to be tolerant of both sides of our nature--knowing that these reflect the twin aspect of the universe--and learn from whatever wisdom is offered.
explicacao: Citação de David Darling sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “No one, from pontiffs to professors, has a monopoly on the truth. In the end, we are all just tra…” articula reflexao, verdade, sabedoria em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como viagem e dia-chuvoso.
